title: EIP-8099: MEVless Protocol
description: Introduces blind transaction sequencing to prevent MEV attacks. Block production is split into two phases: sequencing blocks that order transactions by hash without revealing content, and execution blocks that run transactions in the predetermined order. This removes validators' ability to perform sandwich attacks, front-running, and back-running during the ordering phase.
author: Lawliet Chan (@lawliet-chan)
laymanDescription: Prevents validators from exploiting users by hiding transaction details during the ordering step. Transactions are first sorted by their hash, then revealed and executed in that locked-in order. This stops common attacks like sandwich trades that cost DeFi users money.

endUsers.description: DeFi users would be protected from sandwich attacks and front-running, reducing hidden costs and improving trade execution prices.

appDevs.description: DeFi developers would no longer need to build MEV-protection mechanisms into their applications, simplifying smart contract design.

walletDevs.description: Wallets must support two-phase transaction submission: sending a hash with prepayment first, then submitting full transaction content in a separate step.

toolingInfra.description: Block explorers and mempool monitoring tools must adapt to the two-phase block model, tracking sequencing blocks and execution blocks separately.

layer2s.description: No direct impact on L2 protocols, though L2 sequencers could adopt a similar blind-sequencing pattern independently.

stakersNodes.description: Validators lose MEV revenue from transaction ordering. The protocol relies on unpredictable block production to prevent speculative MEV attacks.

clClients.description: Consensus clients need changes to support alternating sequencing and execution block types, plus randomized block producer selection requirements.

elClients.description: Execution clients must implement two-phase block processing, new RPC methods (eth_sendTxHash, eth_getTxSequence), hash verification, and prepayment gas economics.

benefits: Eliminates sandwich attacks and front-running at the protocol level.
More decentralized than private mempools and more efficient than encrypted mempool approaches.
Reduces hidden costs for DeFi users by removing extractive MEV.
Simplifies DeFi application development by removing need for MEV-protection mechanisms.
tradeoffs: Requires two-phase transaction submission, adding latency and complexity for users and wallets.
Depends on unpredictable block production; less effective with predictable producer selection.
Validators lose MEV-derived revenue, potentially reducing staking incentives.

improveUX.description: Protects users from MEV extraction, reducing hidden transaction costs and improving fairness of trade execution in DeFi.
